entity.name: Mattia Egloff
entity.description: Creator and maintainer of Vauchi — privacy-focused updatable contact cards via in-person QR exchange. E2E encrypted using Signal protocol (X3DH + Double Ratchet), decentralized, no accounts required. Built in Rust with iOS (SwiftUI), Android (Kotlin/Compose), Desktop (Tauri), CLI, and TUI clients.

projects.name: Vauchi
projects.description: Privacy-focused updatable contact cards. Exchange contact information in person via QR code, with end-to-end encryption. When you update your phone number or email, everyone who has your card sees the change — without any central server having access to your data. Uses Signal protocol cryptography (X3DH + Double Ratchet) with an oblivious privacy-preserving relay.

channels.description: Recurring or one-time sponsorship via GitHub Sponsors.
Recurring donations via Liberapay. No platform fees.

plans.name: Full-time development
plans.description: Fund dedicated development of Vauchi: cross-platform apps, relay infrastructure, protocol specification, and security audits.

plans.name: Security audit
plans.description: Fund an external security audit of Vauchi's cryptographic implementation (Signal protocol: X3DH + Double Ratchet in Rust).

plans.name: Relay infrastructure
plans.description: Cover relay server hosting, domain costs, and CI/CD infrastructure.

plans.name: Support Vauchi
plans.description: Contribute any amount to support privacy-respecting open-source software.
